strategy: Hit with Myth or Life, she takes +35% from both, and never attack into her 80% Storm resist.
She opens with a free 0-pip Darkwind global on Round 1 and instantly renews it if you overwrite it, so bring your own damage instead of fighting her global.
Any single-target spell of 3 pips or less at her (cheap attacks, blades on her, traps, weaknesses) makes her interrupt-cast Vampire and stun you for a round. Keep single-target casts at 4+ pips.
Self-target blades are safe. Stack blades on yourself, then hit big.
Traps stick since she never removes them, so if you Feint her expect the one-round stun, then follow with a 4+ pip Myth or Life hit next turn. A stun block helps.
cheatNotes: She casts a free 0-pip Darkwind global on Round 1. It is her buff, not yours to fix.
If you overwrite her global with your own, she instantly re-casts Darkwind for free, do not bother replacing it.
Her minion's Time of Legend global does not count as replacing her global, so it will not trigger the renew.
Pet-cast spells do not trigger the renew, so pet globals are safe.
Cast a single-target spell of 3 pips or less at her and she interrupt-casts Vampire and stuns you for a round. Keep hits and traps at 4+ pips or bring a stun block.
Wand maycast spells do not provoke the Vampire-stun, so wand hits are safe pokes.
Arcane Deduction does trigger the Vampire-stun, so avoid casting it.
Backdraft at 4 or more pips is safe and will not trigger the Vampire-stun.
She never removes traps she is given, so a Feint you land will stay, just plan around the stun when you place it.
